verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane immunoglobulin-superfamily protein expressed exclusively on spermatozoa, essential for sperm-egg fusion via binding to JUNO on the oocyte. It has its own TM anchor and an extracellular Ig-like ectodomain, but surface display is restricted to the male germline lineage (post-acrosome-reaction sperm), making this tissue/lineage-restricted rather than a broadly accessible classical surface receptor. Maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface per the rule that germline-restricted display with its own anchor is contextual, not yes.
